subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: Explain the structure and functions of United Nations affiliates
List the achievements of the United Nations
Recall the achievements of the World Health Organization
Recall the services of FAO, ILO, UNICEF, UNESCO
Understand the role of IMF, IBRD and WTO in global economy

key_terms.definition: International organization established in 1945 to maintain peace and security
The World Parliament of the UN — all member states, one vote each
The Cabinet of UN — 15 members including 5 permanent members with veto power
World Health Organization — headquartered in Geneva, founded in 1948
Food and Agriculture Organization — headquartered in Rome, Italy
Organization established for welfare of children after World War II
United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
International Monetary Fund — called the central bank of central banks
World Trade Organization — third economic pillar of world trade and commerce
South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation — headquarters in Kathmandu, established 1985

mcqs.question: The United Nations was established in the year
mcqs.explanation: The United Nations was established on October 24, 1945

mcqs.question: The headquarters of the United Nations is located in
mcqs.explanation: The headquarters of the United Nations is in New York, USA

mcqs.question: The subsidiary body called the 'Cabinet of UN' is
mcqs.explanation: The Security Council is called the Cabinet of the UN as it is the most powerful executive body

mcqs.question: The headquarters of the World Health Organization is located in
mcqs.explanation: WHO headquarters is in Geneva, Switzerland

mcqs.question: The organ of UN known as 'World Parliament' is
mcqs.explanation: The General Assembly is called the World Parliament as all member states participate

mcqs.question: World Health Organization was founded in the year

one_mark_questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the Food and Agriculture Organization located?
Which organization was established after World War II for the welfare of children?
Which organization is called the Central Bank of the Central Banks of various nations?
Where is the headquarters of the European Union located?
The headquarters of the International Court of Justice is located in

three_mark_questions.question: Which are the organs of the United Nations?
What are the aims and objectives of the United Nations?
answer: To maintain international peace and security
To develop mutual understanding among nations
To promote faith in fundamental human rights
To find cooperative solutions to international economic, social, cultural and humanitarian problems
To ensure the observance of international law
To act as a centre of mutual understanding among nations

three_mark_questions.question: What are the objectives of the Food and Agriculture Organization?
answer: Liberation of the global population from hunger
Improvement of the living standards of people in rural areas
